semantic.role: CLI command handler for the 'try' subcommand. Orchestrates a three-stage operation (repository posture validation, language landscape discovery, and AST preview generation) by delegating entirely to the TryService.
responsibilities: Parse and validate CLI arguments for the 'try' command, providing usage instructions on failure.
Delegate to TryService.checkRepoPosture to validate the repository state.
Delegate to TryService.discoverLanguageLandscape to detect programming languages used in the project.
Delegate to TryService.generateASTPreviews to produce AST samples for detected languages.
Format and print the combined result (posture, languages, AST previews) to the console via console.log.
Accept optional banner callback for consistent UI display across commands.
outOfScope: Persistence of any kind (database writes, file storage).
Pipeline orchestration or multi-step sequencing beyond the single try command.
Cross-command state management or runtime session coordination.
Architectural rule checking or validation logic (those belong in engines).
invariants: Must remain a thin entrypoint: all business logic belongs in services/engines/infra, never absorbed into this module.
Must always validate arguments via throwCliUsage before performing any service call.
Must delegate checkRepoPosture, discoverLanguageLandscape, and generateASTPreviews to the TryService — never implement them locally.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Implement the 'try' CLI command as a thin entrypoint that delegates to a dedicated service layer, maintaining separation between CLI adapters and business logic.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Part of a broader refactoring that removed legacy CLI aliases, rebuilt documentation, fixed schemas, and updated tests. No semantic change to this file's contract.

publicSurface.description: Options interface for the try command handler, providing CLI arguments, project root directory, and an optional banner display callback. Used by the CLI framework to invoke the command.
Main entry point for the 'try' CLI command. It validates arguments, calls TryService to check repo posture, discover languages, and generate AST previews, then prints results to stdout.
